CREATE TABLE documents (id INT, content TEXT);
CREATE TABLE authors (id INT, name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE tags (id INT, tag_name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO documents (id, content) VALUES (1, 'This is a sample document for testing.');
INSERT INTO authors (id, name) VALUES (1, 'John Doe');
INSERT INTO documents (id, content) VALUES (2, 'Another test document with some text for scoring.');
SELECT bif:score() FROM documents WHERE content CONTAINS 'test';
SELECT d.id, bif:score() FROM documents d JOIN authors a ON d.id = a.id WHERE d.content CONTAINS 'document';
SELECT d.id FROM documents d WHERE bif:score() > 0.5;
SELECT a.name, bif:score() FROM authors a JOIN documents d ON a.id = d.id WHERE d.content CONTAINS 'for';
SELECT d.id, bif:score() AS score FROM documents d WHERE d.content CONTAINS 'sample' ORDER BY score DESC;
SELECT d.id FROM (SELECT bif:score() AS score, id FROM documents WHERE content CONTAINS 'test') AS sub WHERE score > 0.5;
SELECT d.id, COUNT(t.id) AS tag_count FROM documents d LEFT JOIN tags t ON d.id = t.id GROUP BY d.id HAVING bif:score() > 0.4;
SELECT d.content FROM documents d WHERE bif:score() IN (SELECT bif:score() FROM documents WHERE content CONTAINS 'some');
